channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: What service managers can highlight during childcare centre tours in 2026
item.description: For many families, a childcare centre tour is the moment they decide whether a service feels safe, welcoming and right for their child.
item.content:encoded: For many families, a childcare centre tour is the moment they decide whether a service feels safe, welcoming and right for their child.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Federal Budget leaves families, early educators and services in limbo
item.description: National early education peak body Community Early Learning Australia has called for the Federal Government to commit to extending the Worker Retention Payment now, warning that families, services, educators and teachers are being left without certainty.
item.content:encoded: National early education peak body Community Early Learning Australia has called for the Federal Government to commit to extending the Worker Retention Payment now, warning that families, services, educators and teachers are being left without certainty.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Building leadership pathways the next generation of ECEC professionals wants to step into - The Sector
item.description: Building leadership pathways the next generation of ECEC professionals wants to step into - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Building leadership pathways the next generation of ECEC professionals wants to step into - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Annual Wage Review 2026: what the latest FWC update means for the ECEC sector
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has released new Annual Wage Review 2026 material, with the outcome set to influence educator wages, workforce retention and service viability across early childhood education and care.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission has released new Annual Wage Review 2026 material, with the outcome set to influence educator wages, workforce retention and service viability across early childhood education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Turning the corner isn’t enough: ECEC now needs system conditions that stick
item.description: For years, early childhood education and care (ECEC) has been defined by pressure: workforce shortages, rising costs, reform fatigue and operational instability.
item.content:encoded: For years, early childhood education and care (ECEC) has been defined by pressure: workforce shortages, rising costs, reform fatigue and operational instability.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Budget 2026–27: ECEC sector welcomes stability but warns gaps are growing faster than funding
item.description: The 2026–27 Federal Budget has delivered modest movement for early childhood education and care (ECEC), prompting sector feedback that can best be described as measured, cautious and increasingly concerned about the gap between policy ambition and operational reality.
item.content:encoded: The 2026–27 Federal Budget has delivered modest movement for early childhood education and care (ECEC), prompting sector feedback that can best be described as measured, cautious and increasingly concerned about the gap between policy ambition and operational reality.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.description: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.content:encoded: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.dc:creator: Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: New video series supports positive behaviour guidance and child-safe practice
item.description: A new series of regulatory priority videos has been released to support early childhood education and care (ECEC) professionals to strengthen everyday interactions with children.ons, family engagement and curriculum design.
item.content:encoded: A new series of regulatory priority videos has been released to support early childhood education and care (ECEC) professionals to strengthen everyday interactions with children.ons, family engagement and curriculum design.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver
